title: Discrete Survival Hazard Features
summary: PitNextLap is a next-event prediction after stint reset, matching a discrete-time hazard framing.
rationale: PitNextLap is a next-event prediction after stint reset, matching a discrete-time hazard framing. Public F1 modelling describes pit stops as structural resets and tyre degradation as a latent state, while current features mostly use static ratios rather than empirical survival curves.
implementation_hint: Estimate OOF smoothed hazards P(PitNextLap=1 | Compound, Stint, TyreLife_bin, RaceProgress_bin), plus cumulative survival proxy up to current TyreLife and hazard deltas from previous/next bins. Use monotonic smoothing or Bayesian m-estimates to avoid sparse-bin noise, and include dry/wet separate hazard tables.
expected_effect: Should give tree models a compact prior for when a tyre/stint typically becomes pit-eligible, improving ranking around ambiguous mid-stint laps.
risk: If bins are too granular, sparse Driver/Race combinations overfit; if folds are random within race, survival priors may still leak strategy patterns.
